title: Pentagon Withheld Dozens of U.S. Military Injuries in Iran War
summary: Central Command is not required to release information about injured troops, especially when they quickly return to duty, a military official said.

title: Inside the Deadly U.S. Airstrike on a Shadow Fleet Tanker
summary: Evidence suggests the ship on which three Indian sailors died was not moving when it was struck in the Gulf of Oman, but it had been engaged in risky offshore transfers of oil.

title: Pentagon Identifies 2 U.S. Soldiers Killed in Jordan During Iran Strike
summary: Both were active-duty soldiers, the Defense Department said. They were killed in action after an Iranian strike on a U.S. base on Friday.

title: How Google’s A.I. Search Is Imperiling the Open Web
summary: As Google incorporates more artificial intelligence into search, people are spending more time on Google. Some website operators are crying foul.

summary: Mobile apps are easy to make with artificial intelligence. That doesn’t mean people are excited to use them.

title: Trump Administration Sought Phone Records of Times Journalists and Their Relatives
summary: The government is aggressively seeking the confidential sources of Times reporters who wrote about the president’s new Air Force One. The Times has asked a judge to quash the subpoenas.

title: Trump Says New Air Force One Will Be Taken Out of Service to Be ‘Maxed Out’
summary: The Qatari-donated plane has been under scrutiny over whether it is fitted with sufficient defensive capabilities to adequately protect a U.S. president and others traveling on it.

title: Judge Temporarily Pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
summary: The delay is intended to give the court time to consider a lawsuit that argues the $111 billion merger violates antitrust laws.

title: Aiming for My Fastest Marathon, I Turned to Distance Running’s Mecca
summary: A New York Times reporter’s quest to beat the personal best he set 14 years ago took him to a Kenyan village, where the biggest benefit he got was not what he expected.

title: In Maine Senate Race, Democrats Go From Chaos to Coronation
summary: Troy Jackson’s emergence as the clear front-runner to replace Graham Platner spares the party some challenges but points to some new ones.

title: 5 Things to Know About the Maine Senate Candidate Troy Jackson
summary: Mr. Jackson, who has emerged as the clear front-runner to replace Graham Platner, is a Republican turned Democrat who is seen as close to Mr. Platner on policy.

title: Spain Celebrates Both a Soccer Win and a Sense of Wider Success
summary: For many fans, the World Cup victory cemented a belief that something promising — a cultural, economic and sports revival — is happening in the country.

title: North Sea Drilling Offers First Test for Burnham’s Relationship With Trump
summary: Andy Burnham has not yet met President Trump, but the two men spoke shortly after Mr. Burnham became prime minister.

title: There’s Nothing Casual About Andy Burnham’s Casual Style
summary: Though it is smart. The new British prime minister is ushering in a new era of Westminster dressing.

title: The ‘Bad Blood’ Between Polymarket’s Shayne Coplan and Kalshi’s Tarek Mansour
summary: Shayne Coplan of Polymarket and Tarek Mansour of Kalshi are young billionaires intent on dominating prediction markets. It’s more than a battle. It’s “bad blood.”

title: Why Epidemics Breed Rage at Health Workers
summary: Some people have fought Ebola containment measures in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Scholars have seen similar responses across societies and throughout history.

title: Soaring Egg Prices Are Hitting China Hard
summary: China consumes more eggs per capita than almost every other country, so a recent spike in costs is touching a nerve.

title: Cinephiles Took Their Own Journeys to See ‘The Odyssey’ in IMAX 70mm
summary: To see Christopher Nolan’s interpretation of an epic journey in his preferred IMAX 70mm format, admirers packed their bags and took out their passports.

summary: Before its release, some in the country were highly skeptical about the new depiction of a beloved tale. But many who saw it over opening weekend came back with positive reviews.

title: To Sell Homer’s ‘Odyssey,’ Book Publishers Get Creative
summary: The market for the epic poem is as crowded as a certain Ithacan palace, and publishers have had to get crafty to attract readers to their editions.

title: This Is Why Our Fresh Produce Keeps Making Us Sick
summary: Cyclospora outbreaks are just a small part of a much larger food safety crisis linked to fresh produce.

title: Man Sets Off Explosive Device Outside 26 Federal Plaza in NYC
summary: The man, who was detained, threatened passers-by, lit a fire and set off fireworks, police said, at the building, which houses an Immigration and Customs Enforcement office.
